differential_diagnoses: Metastatic malignancy with central nervous system involvement
next_steps: Urgent initiation of empirical therapy for fungal and bacterial meningitis (e.g., liposomal amphotericin B, ceftriaxone, and vancomycin)
Endocrine workup including serum cortisol, ACTH, and 24-hour urine free cortisol/catecholamines
Repeat CSF analysis for fungal, mycobacterial, and viral PCR testing, including India ink and fungal/bacterial cultures
Systemic imaging review for occult malignancy (e.g., PET-CT) if stable
Close neurological monitoring and intracranial pressure management given the elevated opening pressure
Consider diagnostic biopsy of the adrenal lesion if the endocrine workup confirms hypercortisolism
Urgent initiation of empirical therapy for fungal and bacterial meningitis including liposomal amphotericin B and vancomycin/ceftriaxone
Plasma fractionated metanephrines and normetanephrines to rule out pheochromocytoma before further adrenal intervention
CSF cytology and flow cytometry to evaluate for malignancy
CSF testing for cryptococcal antigen, fungal culture, AFB smear/culture, and TB PCR
Endocrine workup including serum cortisol, ACTH, and DHEA-S
Consultation with neurosurgery/neurology for VNS device safety protocols to enable urgent brain MRI
Brain imaging with MRI (if feasible after VNS interrogation) to evaluate for intracranial infection or metastatic spread
